reasoning: With only ~65 days remaining until the December 31, 2025 deadline, comprehensive searches reveal no recent pledges of HIMARS, M270, PULS, or Chunmoo systems to Ukraine by European countries in 2025. While there is a significant push for Europe to bolster its defense funding (e.g., the proposed €40 billion plan), European nations are currently struggling to procure these MRLS systems even for their own depleted forces, facing long lead times and production bottlenecks from the US and South Korea (e.g., Estonia and Poland complaining about delays). Furthermore, the US resumed some deliveries of weapons including GMLRS around July 2025, lightly relieving the immediate pressure on European allies to substitute launchers. Considering the extremely short time horizon and the absence of specific rumors or scheduled announcements for rocket artillery, the historical base rate of such pledges within a 2-month window is low. I am forecasting substantially below the recent market estimate of 43%.
